name: Alvin Batiste
abstract: Alvin Batiste Sr. (November 7, 1932 – May 6, 2007) was an American avant-garde jazz clarinetist, who was born in New Orleans, Louisiana, United States.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Donny Hathaway/ Herbie Mann Quintet and The Family of Mann

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Turbinton
about_quotes.evidence: I learned from Alvin Batiste, who's my teacher during that time

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: when I finished from Dillard I had been playing locally with Edward Blackwell, Harold Batiste, Alvin Batiste, who was a clarinet player
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: You had a chance to study with Alvin Batiste... It was great because of the way Alvin…. Alvin’s thing was about making you think
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: We started to play as a quintet with Alvin Batiste playing clarinet and Harold Battiste playing tenor saxophone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kidd Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: But fortunately I was with Alvin Batiste who was one of the great clarinet players... I was on the first seat sitting next to Alvin so I had the last laugh on all of them, so through the rest of my university time I was sitting on the front seat with Alvin
about_quotes.doc_title: Kidd Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I was telling people like Billy Taylor, who was doing some of that stuff, Alvin Batiste, and David Baker was doing that also at that time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
